# Jake Cuth.

> Editorial-style portfolio of live, interactive data and ML projects by Jacob Cuthbertson, a senior data analyst. The site emphasizes real receipts (no synthetic data), demonstrating concepts rather than describing them, and an editorial tone modeled after publications like the FT and NYT Upshot.

The site is hand-coded vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript with no build step, and no backend except a single Cloudflare Pages Function for the model-picker lab. Every interactive lab runs entirely in the browser. Traffic is measured with privacy-respecting Cloudflare Web Analytics (no cookies, no fingerprinting, no third-party trackers). The site has 36 active lab pages organized into six categories (Statistical inference, Interactive ML, AI & economy, Paper anatomies, Algorithm primers, Shipped builds), filterable from the homepage at /#research, plus a `/notes/` blog section updated three times per week.

## Statistical inference (4)

- [A/B Test Simulator](https://jakecuth.com/work/ab-test-lab/): Bayesian and frequentist A/B test simulator with adjustable priors. Demonstrates why peeking inflates the false-positive rate from 5% nominal to 26% empirical.
- [Customer Segmentation](https://jakecuth.com/work/segmentation-lab/): K-means and DBSCAN clustering on 1M UK retail transactions with RFM features and silhouette scoring.
- [Churn Model](https://jakecuth.com/work/churn-lab/): Predicts which customers will cancel using gradient-boosted decision trees on telecom data, with SHAP feature importances and a fairness audit by demographic.
- [Influencer Mix Modeling](https://jakecuth.com/work/imm-lab/): A working Bayesian Marketing Mix Model for a creator-driven D2C brand. 8 channels, 104 weeks, 200 posterior draws.

## Interactive ML (3)

- [Sketch Recognition](https://jakecuth.com/work/sketch-lab/): A 30-class sketch classifier running entirely in your browser via ONNX. ~930 KB model, no API calls.
- [Model Picker](https://jakecuth.com/work/model-picker-lab/): RAG over OpenRouter's 300+ model catalog. Ask in plain language, get a recommendation plus a side-by-side of four models that fit.
- [The Model Atlas](https://jakecuth.com/work/model-atlas-lab/): An expert system over 13 discriminative ML models. Five questions about your problem, one recommendation with reasons, and deep live demos for every algorithm.

## AI & economy (10)

- [AI & Jobs](https://jakecuth.com/work/jobs-lab/): Type any U.S. occupation. See its AI exposure, robotics exposure, and BLS projection through 2030 across 888 occupations.
- [Time-Use Atlas](https://jakecuth.com/work/time-use-lab/): How Americans actually spend 24 hours, drawn as a clock. Pick an age, employment status, or parenting situation; watch the day redraw.
- [Algorithm Watchdog](https://jakecuth.com/work/watchdog-lab/): Daily-refreshed dashboard of what YouTube's algorithm is rewarding right now. Video length, posting times, category mix, channel concentration.
- [AGI Horizon](https://jakecuth.com/work/agi-forecast-lab/): 250 public AGI predictions from 1950 to 2026, plotted on year said vs year targeted. Shows that AGI has been "twenty years out" since 1965.
- [The Productivity Mystery](https://jakecuth.com/work/productivity-lab/): Why measured U.S. labor productivity has stagnated 1947 to 2025, told through seven public datasets and seven candidate explanations.
- [How LLMs Learn](https://jakecuth.com/work/llm-learning-lab/): The four-stage pipeline from a blank model to ChatGPT, drawn as a single annotated chain. Scaling laws, training stack, tokenization, alignment.
- [Escape Velocity](https://jakecuth.com/work/escape-velocity-lab/): Six measurable curves arguing whether AI is approaching escape velocity or riding an S-curve. Built with Kimi K2.6.
- [Semiconductor Cartography](https://jakecuth.com/work/semiconductor-lab/): The geography of the AI chip supply chain. Thirty facilities, seven layers, the chokepoints that could stall the industry. Built with Kimi K2.6.
- [Tech Predictions](https://jakecuth.com/work/tech-predictions-lab/): A century of confident technology predictions, scored against what actually shipped.
- [Productivity Predictions](https://jakecuth.com/work/productivity-predictions-lab/): Ninety-one confident, dated, named predictions about when IT and AI will show up in BLS productivity statistics. Built with Kimi K2.6.

## Paper anatomies (4)

- [Inside DeepSeek-V4](https://jakecuth.com/work/deepseek-v4-lab/): Five things most people assume modern LLMs do, and what DeepSeek-V4 does instead. Compressed sparse attention, hyper-connections, the Muon optimizer, FP4 quantization-aware training. Authored by Claude Code from the V4 technical report.
- [Inside Subquadratic](https://jakecuth.com/work/subquadratic-lab/): Anatomy of the May 5, 2026 SubQ AI launch. Real $29M seed and $19.6M GPU contract, third-party-verified production benchmarks, self-reported research-mode claims of 12M context and 1,000x compute reduction, and the Magic.dev parallel that triggered the AI Theranos question. Nine-claim smell-test scorecard.
- [Attention Is All You Need](https://jakecuth.com/work/attention-lab/): An interactive infographic of the 2017 Transformer paper. Hand-tuned 6x6 attention heatmap, eight learned head patterns, and a complexity slider that demonstrates why self-attention beats RNNs whenever sequence length n is smaller than representation dimension d.
- [Inside LeWorldModel](https://jakecuth.com/work/leworldmodel-lab/): A 15M-parameter JEPA world model that replaces seven anti-collapse loss terms with one Gaussian-matching regularizer (SIGReg). Single 192-dim CLS token per frame, 48x faster planning than DINO-WM, end-to-end trainable on a single GPU.

## Algorithm primers (13)

- [Logistic Regression](https://jakecuth.com/work/logistic-regression-lab/): The simplest classifier worth deploying. Plant points on a canvas, watch gradient descent slide a probability boundary into place live.
- [Linear Regression](https://jakecuth.com/work/linear-regression-lab/): Fitting a line. Drag points, watch coefficients update.
- [Ridge & Lasso](https://jakecuth.com/work/ridge-lasso-lab/): Two regularizers side by side. Watch coefficients shrink, and Lasso zero them out.
- [Decision Tree](https://jakecuth.com/work/decision-tree-lab/): Recursive splits made visible. Depth slider shows overfit and underfit.
- [Random Forest](https://jakecuth.com/work/random-forest-lab/): Bagging in motion. Many imperfect trees beat one perfect one.
- [Gradient Boosting](https://jakecuth.com/work/gradient-boosting-lab/): Each tree fixes the last tree's mistakes. The technique behind XGBoost.
- [Support Vector Machine](https://jakecuth.com/work/svm-lab/): Soft-margin linear SVM with support vectors highlighted. Live SGD on hinge loss.
- [Multi-Layer Perceptron](https://jakecuth.com/work/mlp-lab/): A small neural net with backprop, one batch at a time.
- [Naive Bayes](https://jakecuth.com/work/naive-bayes-lab/): Conditional independence drawn as overlapping clouds. Wrong assumption, surprisingly useful answer.
- [K-Nearest Neighbors](https://jakecuth.com/work/knn-lab/): No training, just measure distance to k neighbors and vote.
- [K-Means](https://jakecuth.com/work/kmeans-lab/): Pick k, watch points snap to centroids. Lloyd's algorithm, drawn live.
- [DBSCAN](https://jakecuth.com/work/dbscan-lab/): Density-based clustering, no k needed. Eps and minPts control what counts as a cluster.
- [Isolation Forest](https://jakecuth.com/work/isolation-forest-lab/): Random splits, short paths to anomalies.

## Shipped builds (2)

- [Recommendation Engine](https://jakecuth.com/work/reco-lab/): A live, in-browser movie recommender. Click posters you like and watch three different algorithms (collaborative filtering, content-based, hybrid) return different answers.
- [Funnel Simulation](https://jakecuth.com/work/funnel-sim-lab/): Agent-based simulation of a marketing funnel. Hundreds of dots flow through five stages with per-segment dropoff rates.

## Notes

- [Notes index](https://jakecuth.com/notes/) — Field notes from a senior data analyst, three a week.
- [Atom feed](https://jakecuth.com/feed.xml) — Subscribe to notes.
